recommendations.name: Swaddle blankets
recommendations.curator_note: Hospital doesn't recommend swaddling after you leave. Some babies like it though. Don't get too many. We really liked the ones that came with the nanit (easy to use).

recommendations.name: Sleep sacks
recommendations.curator_note: Ideally have 2 on rotation since it can get dirty at night if there are accidents. 1.0 tog is good enough. Or better yet - Woolino is awesome because you dont have to worry about temperature control.

recommendations.name: Breast pump
recommendations.curator_note: $300 covered by insurance, needed prescription from OB. Used medelo freestyle and really liked it.

recommendations.name: High chair
recommendations.curator_note: We did bugaboo giraffe and really like it - easy to clean and easy to adjust footrest. Don't get stokke, food gets stuck in it, hard to adjust (need allen key).

recommendations.name: Baby bottles
recommendations.curator_note: West coast kids bottle sampler or just buy doctor browns with flow 1 for nipple. Have a couple bottles at the beginning.

recommendations.name: Bottle cleaning brush
recommendations.curator_note: Should be separate from the one you use to clean dishes. Also need a separate bowl for baby things in the sink.

recommendations.name: Bottle sterilizer
recommendations.curator_note: Necessary. I have Dr Browns. Wait for it to go on sale. Better yet: splurge for a bottle washer and sterilizer (I might do this for baby #2 becuse at one point i was washing 6 bottles a day and it sucked).

recommendations.name: Bottle warmer
recommendations.curator_note: Probably a nice to have but we used this a lot. Cold bottles are hard if your baby is used to breastfeeding. But over time make the bottle less hot so your baby gets used to room temp / cold bottles. makes it so much easier (especially on the go / while travelling).

recommendations.name: Drying rack
recommendations.curator_note: Don't get the grass one, it gets really icky as water pools in the bottom and is harder to clean. Get one that allows you to stand / stack up. I would put it in the dishwasher periodically.

recommendations.name: Breast milk storage
recommendations.curator_note: This medela kit has been suuuper handy. Comes with different size storage containers, ability to label bottles for tracking, and plastic bags for freezing.

recommendations.name: Formula Pro
recommendations.curator_note: Don't buy this unless you know you're going to be using formula. We have it now and loveeee it but it wasn't needed for 6 months.

recommendations.name: Formula
recommendations.curator_note: Have on hand before baby arrives in case of feeding issues. We use kendamil from Costco. European formulas are supposed to be cleaner? Every baby is different and will respond differently to each one. We know a number of peopel that have used Kendamil successfully though. Also at Walmart.

recommendations.name: Diaper bag
recommendations.curator_note: Love this bag (Mina Baie). Nylon > leather because otherwise the leather is too heavy with all the stuff you pack into it.

recommendations.name: Backseat mirror / camera
recommendations.curator_note: Get one with a soft edge... tbd if mirror is better than camera but starting with mirror. Some people said sun burned a hole in their cars fabric?? Have never experienced anything close to this.

recommendations.name: Stroller
recommendations.curator_note: Personal preference: Bugaboo donkey and bugaboo butterfly. I love having both for different purposes. Could go on about this. Ask if you're curious!

recommendations.name: Infant car seat / convertible car seat
recommendations.curator_note: Clek Ling, highest safety rating and made in Canada.

recommendations.name: Baby carrier
recommendations.curator_note: Get one that clips on/off... the scarf things take too long to put on in my opinion and i find the strap/clippy ones are easier for dads too.

recommendations.name: Baby bouncer
recommendations.curator_note: Baby bjorn stores up nice and small too which we love. Also have one that vibrates that was a hand-me down though that she really likes.

recommendations.name: Baby play gym / activity mat
recommendations.curator_note: In retrospect would by the fisher price one where they lay down and have the feet kicking the piano (would get this for baby number 2).

recommendations.name: Baby lounger
recommendations.curator_note: Snuggle Me - wasn't going to buy this but everyone said I needed it. My daughter hated it. An expensive pillow that just sits in a bag.... if you know someone who has it, try it before investing. Also apparently they are discontinuing for safety reasons in the US now... would pass.

recommendations.name: Floor mat (for playing)
recommendations.curator_note: I got the ones that come in puzzle pieces which was nice as when she was little I could move sections around to different parts of the house (eg. brought one downstairs to my workout mat when she would just lay there).

recommendations.name: Play pen
recommendations.curator_note: Uppa baby remi - LOVE this. It has a change table, and bassinet setting. perfect for the main floor (and compact), and perfect for travelling to cottage/ottawa. Can grow into it as a playpen in future. We used it as a playpen and crib while travelling. This thing has paid for itself so many times over. Would 1000% recommend this as a necessary item.

recommendations.name: Portable sound machine
recommendations.curator_note: Hatch on the go. Portable sound machine is necessary. Life saver. Very long battery life.

recommendations.name: Car seat cozy
recommendations.curator_note: Necessary for winter. Love the one we have from 7am Enfant.

recommendations.name: Car seat protector
recommendations.curator_note: I dont think you need this.

recommendations.name: Stroller muffs
recommendations.curator_note: Personal pref... i like having gloves better for dog walking alongside stroller so i never got this and don't think I will.

recommendations.name: Stroller fan
recommendations.curator_note: Momcozy. Can use at hospital during birth too. Used this a lot in the summer both on stroller and in car (air doesnt circulate as well in the car when the car seat cover is fully retracted during naps so this helped keep her cool).

recommendations.name: Baby toothbrush
recommendations.curator_note: FridaBaby.

recommendations.name: Baby bathtub
recommendations.curator_note: Cheese grater one is great until they can sit up, then recommend switching to effectively a small bathtub within the bathtub.

recommendations.name: Baby hairbrush / dry skin brush
recommendations.curator_note: Frida baby.

recommendations.name: Baby nail file / clippers
recommendations.curator_note: Frida nail file didn't work. Use the dremmel from Amazon instead it's great. After 6 months switched to frida baby nail clippers which is much easier (was starting to take too long with the dremmel).

recommendations.name: Bath thermometer
recommendations.curator_note: Preferred the one with the actual temp on it. The ducky isn't obvious when it's too hot.

recommendations.name: Wipe warmer
recommendations.curator_note: Not really necessary... but i like the munchkin one that i bought. the wipes get cold quickly if you dont use it as soon as you take it out though. i like that it has a nightlight on it though.

recommendations.name: Penaten cream
recommendations.curator_note: Get this day 1 in prep for potential diaper rash. Recommended by my pediatrician after a realllyyyy bad rash a few days in. Works wonders. We use it daily still.

recommendations.name: Wet bag
recommendations.curator_note: or just use dog poo bags / plastic bags..

recommendations.name: Receiving blankets
recommendations.curator_note: Didn't use this at the hospital though I thought I would.

recommendations.name: Muslin cloths
recommendations.curator_note: You need a million.

recommendations.name: Diaper caddy
recommendations.curator_note: Not a must have.. we didn't use ours too much (just set up a station on the main floor so we didn't have to go upstairs every time).

recommendations.name: Baby wipes
recommendations.curator_note: Huggies or Costco were our pref. Costco is best bang for buck.

recommendations.name: Baby thermometer
recommendations.curator_note: Don't be like me and get the touchless because it "doesn't wake baby, can do it while sleeping." — Doctor prefers the armpit one because it's more reliable and it's cheaper. I agree.

recommendations.name: First aid kit
recommendations.curator_note: Ddrops, tylenol, Frida baby windy (get it - when you need it you will be so happy you have it).

recommendations.name: Baby CPR course
recommendations.curator_note: Recommend only doing this closer to when they start solids (4 months) so it's fresh in your mind!

recommendations.name: Nose cleaner (NoseFrida)
recommendations.curator_note: Frida Baby. Very useful. Have yet to use their snot sucker though.

recommendations.name: Crib mattress
recommendations.curator_note: Breathable. Newton was recommended but i think they are all fine (using ikea one too).

recommendations.name: Crib sheets
recommendations.curator_note: Need more than 1 in case of night time accidents -- they can have leaks / blowouts even at a young age.

recommendations.name: Crib waterproof cover
recommendations.curator_note: Newton waterproof mattress cover.

recommendations.name: Glider chair
recommendations.curator_note: Was recommended motorized to avoid waking baby and bc it's easier on your body.... However - I have almost never reclined in it... i just rock her in it and you can't rock when it's reclined. I don't sleep in the chair so I would say you dont need to spend up for motorized.

recommendations.name: Diaper disposal
recommendations.curator_note: Diaper Genie bc it's the best at retaining odours and has a foot pump. Also taller (no bending down like the ubbi). I find the ubbi hard to open.

recommendations.name: Bassinet
recommendations.curator_note: Only need for a few months. Borrowing instead of buying.

recommendations.name: Baby monitor
recommendations.curator_note: Nanit - Prime day deal. Get with travel stand - easier to move around the house or bring to others places. Also cheaper than the wall mount and heavy stand, you just need a shelf near the crib for it.

recommendations.name: White noise machine / night light
recommendations.curator_note: Hatch rest+. Love this. And it's portable in the room so you don't have to turn on lights to change or feed.

recommendations.name: Change table top
recommendations.curator_note: Peanut Changer. So easy to clean.

recommendations.name: Blackout curtains
recommendations.curator_note: We didn't need these until like 3 months old. Then we reallyyy needed them to help her connect sleep cycles!!
